message:
chore(beads): advance bd-uah perf comparison, update bd-3dxz timestamps

Update issue tracker following performance optimization and stress test
work:

- bd-uah (compare extension performance vs legacy pi baseline): ongoing
  work — memchr truncation optimization and startup reordering landed
- bd-3dxz (memory profiling and 1-hour stress test): timestamp refresh
  from stress test report generation and perf baseline collection

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1mq","title":"Unit tests: CLI/main selection + system prompt building","description":"Goal:
- Add unit tests for core CLI/main.rs logic without mocks (real temp dirs + config fixtures) and with detailed logging.

Scope:
- Model selection + thinking resolution (select_model_and_thinking) across provider/model edge cases.
- System prompt assembly (build_system_prompt) including project context files and skills prompt.
- File argument handling (prepare_initial_message) for text + images.
- API key resolution fallbacks and error paths.

Logging Requirements:
- Use the shared TestHarness/TestLogger (bd-3ml) in each test.
- On failure, log inputs, resolved model selection, and prompt fragments.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests cover normal + error paths and do not use fake providers.
- Uses real filesystem temp dirs for context files.
- Logs include inputs, derived values, and assertion context.
- Deterministic, no network access.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1nn","title":"Audit test coverage + gap matrix (no mocks policy)","description":"# Goal
Audit test coverage and produce a **no-mocks coverage gap matrix** with explicit remediation links.

# Scope
- Inventory existing unit/integration/E2E tests by module and feature area.
- Identify remaining mocks/fakes and classify whether they are acceptable (with rationale).
- Produce a matrix that maps feature areas to tests + evidence artifacts.

# Logging / Artifacts
- Emit JSONL logs (bd-4u9) with scan summary, discovered gaps, and links to beads.
- Output a deterministic artifact index and the updated matrix file.

# Deliverables
- Updated `docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md` (or equivalent) with clear status and next actions.
- List of gaps mapped to bead ids (existing or newly created).

# Acceptance Criteria
- Matrix clearly shows coverage status per module and excludes unapproved mocks.
- Gaps are mapped to beads with owners and dependencies.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1no3","title":"Phase 3: Differential Test Runner (compare TS vs Rust output)","description":"# Phase 3: Differential Test Runner (compare TS vs Rust output)

## Purpose
This is the CORE of the conformance system. The differential test runner:
1. Takes an extension path and a mock spec
2. Runs it through the TS runtime (via Bun subprocess)
3. Runs it through the Rust runtime (via library call)
4. Compares the outputs
5. Reports PASS (identical) or FAIL (with diff)

## Why A Runner (Not Just Tests)
Individual test functions are fine for small numbers of extensions. But we have 200+ extensions to test. The runner automates:
- Extension discovery from the validated manifest
- Mock spec selection (per-extension or default)
- Parallel execution (both runtimes concurrently)
- Structured output comparison
- Report generation

## Runner Architecture

```
ConformanceRunner
  |
  +-- for each extension in manifest:
  |     |
  |     +-- Run TS harness (Bun subprocess) -> ts_output.json
  |     +-- Run Rust harness (library call) -> rust_output.json
  |     +-- Compare(ts_output, rust_output) -> diff_result
  |     +-- Record result
  |
  +-- Generate report: {total, passed, failed, skipped, diffs: [...]}
```

## Comparison Strategy
Not just byte-equality. Semantic comparison that:
- Ignores key ordering in JSON objects
- Handles numeric precision differences (f64 vs TS number)
- Normalizes string whitespace where appropriate
- Groups differences by category (registration, event, hostcall)
- Provides human-readable diff output

## Output
- Per-extension: PASS/FAIL with detailed diff
- Aggregate: summary report with pass rate per tier
- Machine-readable: JSON report for CI
- Human-readable: markdown report for review","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:50.307953690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:34:53.485560824Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:34:53.485496845Z","close_reason":"Differential test runner implemented in tests/ext_conformance_diff.rs. Runner: discovers extensions from VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json, runs TS oracle (Bun + load_extension.ts), runs Rust runtime (QuickJS), compares outputs with diff_snapshots(). All 60 official extensions pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1no3","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:50.327770840Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":311,"issue_id":"bd-1no3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN DECISION: Why Rust integration test (not standalone binary)?

1. CARGO TEST INTEGRATION: Each extension as a #[test] function = automatic parallelism, filtering, and CI integration.
2. LIBRARY ACCESS: Can call Rust extension runtime directly without subprocess overhead.
3. EXISTING PATTERN: conformance_fixtures.rs already uses this pattern with fixture_test! macro.
4. REPORTING: cargo test --format json gives structured output for CI.

The TS side runs as a Bun subprocess because we cannot embed the TS runtime in Rust. This is fine -- the subprocess produces JSON on stdout, which is trivial to parse. The overhead is ~50ms per subprocess spawn, acceptable for 200 extensions.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:17Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1o6l","title":"Extension load-time benchmarks (all 60 official, TS vs Rust)","description":"# Extension load-time benchmarks (all 60 official, TS vs Rust)

## What
For every official extension, measure load time in both runtimes. The differential runner already captures load_time_ms -- this task aggregates and analyzes the results.

## Analysis
- P50, P95, P99 load times for each runtime
- Per-extension comparison table
- Identify outliers (extensions much slower in Rust)
- Root cause for slow Rust loads (swc compilation? QuickJS init? module resolution?)

## Target
- P99 < 100ms for Rust
- Rust <= 2x TypeScript for any extension
- Rust median <= TypeScript median

## Output
- Benchmark report: tests/ext_conformance/reports/load_time_benchmark.json
- Visualization-ready data","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:17.634261428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:28:29.920701184Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:28:29.920635040Z","close_reason":"Added TS harness + Rust timed load capture in tests/ext_conformance_diff.rs; generated tests/ext_conformance/reports/load_time_benchmark.json via load_time_benchmark_official","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o6l","depends_on_id":"bd-139x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:17.799558510Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o6l","depends_on_id":"bd-150s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:17.838617525Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-318h","title":"Task: Implement tool_result event dispatch (modifying)","description":"# Task: Implement tool_result Event Dispatch (Modifying)

## Objective

Dispatch the tool_result event after tool execution, allowing extensions to modify or observe results.

## Background

The tool_result event enables:
- Logging: Record all tool outputs
- Transformation: Modify content/details before returning to model
- Error enrichment: Add context to errors

## TypeScript Reference (wrapper.ts:74-90)

```typescript
if (runner.hasHandlers("tool_result")) {
    const resultResult = await runner.emit({
        type: "tool_result",
        toolName: tool.name,
        toolCallId,
        input: params,
        content: result.content,
        details: result.details,
        isError: false,
    });

    if (resultResult) {
        return {
            content: resultResult.content ?? result.content,
            details: (resultResult.details ?? result.details) as T,
        };
    }
}
```

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn execute_tool_with_events(
        &self,
        tool_call: &ToolCall,
        event_dispatcher: Option<&EventDispatcher>,
    ) -> Result<ToolOutput> {
        // [tool_call event dispatch - see previous task]
        
        // Execute tool
        let result = match self.execute_tool_raw(tool_call).await {
            Ok(output) => output,
            Err(e) => {
                // Dispatch tool_result for errors too
                if let Some(dispatcher) = event_dispatcher {
                    if dispatcher.has_handlers("tool_result") {
                        let event = ExtensionEvent::ToolResult {
                            tool_name: tool_call.name.clone(),
                            tool_call_id: tool_call.id.clone(),
                            input: tool_call.arguments.clone(),
                            content: vec![ContentBlock::Text(TextContent {
                                text: e.to_string(),
                                text_signature: None,
                            })],
                            details: None,
                            is_error: true,
                        };
                        let _ = dispatcher.dispatch::<ToolResultEventResult>(event).await;
                    }
                }
                return Err(e);
            }
        };
        
        // Dispatch tool_result for success
        let final_result = if let Some(dispatcher) = event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("tool_result") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::ToolResult {
                    tool_name: tool_call.name.clone(),
                    tool_call_id: tool_call.id.clone(),
                    input: tool_call.arguments.clone(),
                    content: result.content.clone(),
                    details: result.details.clone(),
                    is_error: false,
                };
                
                if let Some(modification) = dispatcher.dispatch::<ToolResultEventResult>(event).await? {
                    ToolOutput {
                        content: modification.content.unwrap_or(result.content),
                        details: modification.details.or(result.details),
                    }
                } else {
                    result
                }
            } else {
                result
            }
        } else {
            result
        };
        
        Ok(final_result)
    }
}
```

## Event Data Format

```json
{
    "type": "tool_result",
    "toolName": "read",
    "toolCallId": "call-abc123",
    "input": { "path": "secret.txt" },
    "content": [{ "type": "text", "text": "file contents" }],
    "details": { "lines_read": 10 },
    "isError": false
}
```

## Handler Example (Extension)

```javascript
ctx.on("tool_result", async (event) => {
    if (event.toolName === "read") {
        // Redact sensitive content
        const redacted = event.content.map(c => ({
            ...c,
            text: c.text.replace(/password=.*/gi, "password=[REDACTED]")
        }));
        return { content: redacted };
    }
    return null; // No modification
});
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: No handlers → returns original result
2. Unit test: Handler returns null → returns original
3. Unit test: Handler modifies content → returns modified
4. Unit test: Handler modifies details → returns modified
5. Unit test: Error results also dispatched
6. Integration test: Extension redacts sensitive data

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-tg4w (EventDispatcher)
- Depends on: bd-35hz (tool_call dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] tool_result event dispatched after execution
- [ ] Modifications applied to result
- [ ] Null returns preserve original
- [ ] Error results also trigger event

 {"id":"bd-31f6","title":"Validate pi-mono TS runtime builds and can load extensions via Bun","description":"# Validate pi-mono TS runtime builds and can load extensions via Bun

## Context
pi-mono source at legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/ contains the reference TypeScript extension runtime. The extension loader (packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/loader.ts) uses jiti to load TypeScript extensions. In compiled mode it uses virtualModules; in dev mode it uses aliases.

We have Bun 1.3.8 at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun. The pi-mono package.json likely has workspace deps (@mariozechner/pi-agent-core, @mariozechner/pi-ai, @mariozechner/pi-tui).

## What To Do
1. Check if pi-mono already has node_modules/ installed
2. If not, run bun install in the pi-mono root
3. Try importing the extension loader from packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/loader.ts
4. Call loadExtensions() with the hello.ts extension path
5. Verify it loads successfully and captures the extension registrations (tools, commands, handlers, etc.)
6. Document any issues (missing deps, build errors, etc.)

## Key Files
- legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/package.json (workspace root)
- legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/loader.ts
- legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/types.ts
- tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/hello/hello.ts (test extension)

## Acceptance Criteria
- bun install completes without errors
- loadExtensions(['path/to/hello.ts'], cwd) returns Extension object
- Extension object has expected fields (handlers, tools, commands, flags, shortcuts)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:15:10.348140095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:50.118564024Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:30.262141553Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31f6","depends_on_id":"bd-1v10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:15:12.041830986Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":332,"issue_id":"bd-31f6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DONE - TS oracle harness works. 46 of 60 pass, 13 fail ProviderTransport removed, 1 timeout. Harness at tests/ext_conformance/ts_oracle/load_extension.ts. Needs NODE_PATH. batch_load.sh for bulk.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31hg","title":"Replace manual history navigation with bubbles::List component","description":"Use bubbles::List instead of manual implementation for history navigation in TUI.

## Current State
- Interactive TUI has manual history navigation (up/down arrows)
- Custom implementation in interactive.rs
- Doesn't leverage bubbles::List features

## Benefits of bubbles::List
- Built-in keyboard navigation (up/down/home/end/pgup/pgdn)
- Filtering support (could enable history search)
- Consistent behavior with other charmed_rust components
- Less code to maintain

## Implementation
1. Create HistoryList wrapper around bubbles::List
2. Store input history as List items
3. Wire up to existing up/down key handling
4. Remove manual history_index tracking

## Test Plan
- Unit test: history navigation works as before
- Manual test: verify up/down/home/end work
- Verify no behavior changes for existing users

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs (replace manual history with List)

## Risk
- Low - bubbles::List is battle-tested
- Maintain backwards compatibility with existing keybindings","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:12.322493675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:49:53.503674421Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:49:53.503611003Z","close_reason":"Completed: replace manual input history navigation with bubbles::List-backed HistoryList; update /history output; add regression test; gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31hg","depends_on_id":"bd-20lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:12.330362116Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-320","title":"Implement JS compatibility pipeline (bundle -> runtime)","description":"Background:
- Many extensions are JS/TS; we need a compatibility tier to run them as-is.
- Assume **QuickJS has no WebAssembly**: JS compatibility must be hybrid (QuickJS for JS + wasmtime-backed PiWasm for wasm).

Steps:
- Lock the JS execution strategy for the pinned sample:
  - QuickJS bytecode for JS execution.
  - PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry) for `globalThis.WebAssembly` expectations.
  - JS→WASM is optional/future; do not block sample success on it.
- Implement build pipeline integration + caching by hash (extc/SWC) and artifact metadata.
- Provide/ensure shims for legacy APIs (Node core + globals) map into connector hostcalls.
- Integrate with the QuickJS runtime and connector dispatcher.
- Ensure module loading contract supports shim modules and deterministic resolution.

Acceptance (hard):
- **All 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** (no manual source edits), end-to-end under the conformance/E2E harness.

Notes:
- Node core shims: bd-3d0 + bd-2sr.
- WebAssembly bridge: bd-1ry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

 {"id":"bd-331","title":"Dev UX: diagnostics + trace viewer for extensions","description":"# Goal
Improve **developer UX** with a focused diagnostics and trace viewer for extension runs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Standardized log schema for extension lifecycle + hostcalls.
- Pretty renderer for logs (human-readable summary + JSONL export).
- Error messages include: capability name, scope, and fix hints.

# Why (User Value)
- Faster iteration and fewer support questions for extension authors.
- Makes the Node/Bun-free approach feel *better*, not just smaller.

# Tests
- Snapshot tests for rendered output.
- E2E harness consumes and validates logs.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-331g","title":"Concurrent extension stress test (10+ extensions active)","description":"Create a stress test that verifies correct behavior with many extensions loaded simultaneously. Requirements: 1. Load ALL extensions from the conformance suite simultaneously 2. Dispatch interleaved events to all extensions concurrently 3. Verify: no cross-contamination between extension states 4. Verify: no deadlocks or hangs (5s timeout per dispatch) 5. Verify: all tool/command registrations from all extensions are correctly namespaced 6. Verify: event handler ordering is deterministic. This catches concurrency bugs, namespace collisions, and resource contention between extensions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:20:06.895362696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:20:06.917648959Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-331g","depends_on_id":"bd-3p98","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:20:06.917601411Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
